11.reason: The flag is set to 1 when a valid expansion is detected, but it is never reset to 0 for subsequent characters, causing all later characters to be skipped.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The flag is set to 1 when a valid expansion is detected, but it is never reset to 0 for subsequent characters, causing all later characters to be skipped.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The flag is set to 1 when a valid expansion is detected, but it is never reset to 0 for subsequent characters, causing all later characters to be skipped.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing check that a[i-1] < a[i+1]; also no bounds check for i==0 or i==n-1, and flag is never reset after processing, causing later characters to be skipped]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  flag is never reset to 0, causing subsequent characters to be skipped after the first valid expansion.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This flag is set globally and affects all future characters, not just the current expansion. It causes non-expansion characters after a valid '-' to be skipped incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This flag is set globally and never reset per '-', causing incorrect behavior when multiple '-' exist; also, it doesn't ensure the right character is greater than the left]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When flag==1 (i.e., current char is part of an expansion pattern), this skips printing the character before '-', but also incorrectly skips other non-expansion characters later due to persistent flag.]
